title: Jelok

{{Quote|Jelok made another discovery today while helping Deremot clean up some of the rubble in the chamber. A concealed floor panel revealed a small alcove—within the alcove, Jelok found a flat ingot of dull metal, roughly round and pitted along the heavier end.|Jelok's discovery of the Plaque of Victory, as recorded by Doctor Lancer Brunou in his [[Doctor Lancer Brunou's journal|journal]]|<ref name="AOA" />}}
'''Jelok''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] who served in the [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]] in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]. Assigned to research team [[MS-133]], Jelok was part of an expedition dispatched to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aaris III]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. The team's mission was to conduct an [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeological]] dig in [[City of Kastays|ruins]] found on the planet in an effort to discover why the native [[Species/Legends|species]], the [[Aaris]], had gone extinct. During the [[Standard month/Legends|months]]-long mission, Jelok assisted his colleague, [[Grigor Tansad]], in exploring the [[Place of Kastays]], where he discovered the [[Plaque of Victory]], an inanimate lifeform that influenced the emotions of those beings around it by heightening their suspicions and causing violent behavior.

The Plaque of Victory began to affect the team, causing an outbreak of paranoia. Shortly after, MS-133 came under attack by the primitive descendants of the Aaris. As the team began to take casualties, the expedition leader, [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Lancer Brunou]], ordered the mission to be abandoned. Jelok, feeling that his exploration of the Place of Kastays had reached a critical stage, refused to abandon his research and had to be forcibly removed to the team's [[Unidentified Curich-class shuttle|shuttle]]. That night, the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] was destroyed, stranding MS-133 on the planet. Jelok [[Death/Legends|did not survive]] to be rescued by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'', which arrived in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] in response to a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress call]] sent by Doctor Brunou.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Jelok has been examining the chamber discovered a few days ago. The inside walls are covered in odd pictographs raised from the metal in an intricate bas relief. Each pictograph emits a long and complex series of sounds—beeps, rumbles, notes and chirps—when pressed. Jelok surmises that these pictographs are the key to some kind of language used by the ancient inhabitants of this place.|Doctor Lancer Brunou, noting Jelok's findings in his journal|<ref name="AOA" />}}
[[File:Aaris_III.jpg|thumb|right|Aaris III]]
In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Jelok, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was serving in the [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. He was a [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] and was assigned to [[MS-133]], a research team under the command of [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Lancer Brunou]]. The team was dispatched on an [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeological]] expedition to a series of extensive [[City of Kastays|ruins]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aaris III]] with the intention of discovering the cause behind the extinction of the world's [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]], the [[Aaris]]. Over the course of several [[Standard month/Legends|months]], Jelok assisted Brunou and the rest of the team in exploring the extensive metal ruins.<ref name="AOA" />

On the forty-ninth [[Standard day/Legends|day]] of the mission, one of Jelok's fellow scientists, [[Grigor Tansad]], discovered a [[Place of Kastays|chamber]] within one of the ruined structures. Brunou assigned Jelok, along with two [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] and five [[Soldier/Legends|troopers]], to assist Tansad in exploring the chamber. The small group set up a secondary base camp close to the chamber, as the structure was away from MS-133's primary camp. The team discovered that the chamber was covered in bas-relief [[pictograph]]s that emitted sounds when touched. Jelok believed that the sounds the pictographs emitted were the key to the [[Language/Legends|language]] used by the Aaris, while Tansad was of the opinion that they represented a pictorial dating system and served as the civilization's aural history. Both scientists began to work on their own theories, attempting to translate the pictographs.<ref name="AOA" />

After several [[Time/Legends|days]] of work, Tansad's theory about the pictographs proved to be correct, causing Jelok to abandon his own line of research and assist his fellow scientist. On the sixtieth day of the expedition, Jelok was clearing rubble from the chamber's floor when he discovered a concealed panel that housed a [[Plaque of Victory|metal ingot]]. Brunou took the artifact to be analyzed at the main base camp and discovered that it was composed of material unknown to Imperial science, and that it gave off a faint energy reading. He resolved to send it back to Moff Sarne's [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratories]] on his [[Capital/Legends|capital]], the planet [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], for further testing. The team was unaware of the ingot's true nature; the artifact contained a sentient lifeform that fed off the life-force of living beings. Despite being inanimate, it sought to destroy those around it by manipulating their emotions, heightening their suspicions and turning them violent. The Aaris referred to the artifact as the "Plaque of Victory," and it had been responsible for the [[Aaris civil war|civil war]] that had destroyed their civilization thousands of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] previously.<ref name="AOA" />

[[File:Plaque_of_Victory.jpg|thumb|left|Jelok's discovery, the Plaque of Victory]]
Jelok continued to clear the rubble and debris from the chamber while Tansad endeavored to translate the pictographs, entering the findings on his personal [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]]. On the sixty-first day of the expedition, members of the team began to report feelings of being watched from the surrounding jungle, despite there being no evidence of any other living beings on the planet. As a precaution, the [[Unidentified Imperial lieutenant (MS-133)|Lieutenant]] in command of the security detachment led five [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] into the jungle to investigate. An [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] later, [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire was heard in the distance. When none of the team returned, weapons were issued to the rest of the team. Jelok and Tansad continued to work in the chamber despite the incident. That night, the main base camp was attacked<ref name="AOA" /> by the primitive descendants of the Aaris,<ref name="CSWE" /> resulting in the wounding of a [[Unidentified Imperial technician (MS-133, I)|technician]] and Doctor [[Theda]]. Jelok later reported that Tansad was missing from the secondary camp, and that two more technicians had been [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[Sniper/Legends|sniper]] fire from the jungle. With these setbacks, Brunou ordered Jelok to return to the main camp. Jelok refused, stating that he was close to translating the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] language, and that he had discovered that the name of the chamber they were excavating was the "Place of Kastays." Jelok felt that it was vital his work continue, but Brunou disagreed, ordering the remaining troopers to escort Jelok back to the team's [[Unidentified Curich-class shuttle|shuttle]], while he and the rest of the staff packed up the base camp in readiness to leave the planet.<ref name="AOA" />

That night,<ref name="AOA" /> the Aaris<ref name="CSWE" /> attacked again, destroying the team's [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] and reducing MS-133 to a handful of survivors—Doctors Brunou and Theda, [[Solla Deremot]], two troopers, and a [[Unidentified Imperial technician (MS-133, II)|technician]]. With no way off the planet, they fortified the base camp as best they could and sent a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress signal]] to Kal'Shebbol. The message was intercepted by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]''. In the months since MS-133 had departed Kal'Shebbol,<ref name="AOA" /> the New Republic had [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberated]] the planet.<ref name="tsb">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> By the time the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] had arrived, only Brunou and Deremot remained alive to evacuate. [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]], [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] of the ''FarStar'', authorized an investigation into what had occurred on the planet. Crew members searched the Place of Kastays, where they found Tansad's body hidden in the rubble, with evidence that he had been killed by a blow to the head by a heavy rock. The rock in question was found nearby, along with Tansad's datapad, which noted that he felt that Jelok was jealous of his work.<ref name="AOA" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|He even claimed to have discovered the name the ancients had for the chamber—the Place of Kastays. While he didn't know exactly what it meant, Jelok felt it was proof enough not to abandon his work. I disagreed.|Doctor Lancer Brunou, regarding Jelok's behavior, noted in his journal|<ref name="AOA" />}}
Jelok was a scientist with experience in translating alien languages, and he was able to make intuitive leaps and reasoned hypotheses when confronted with imagery from alien civilizations. During the Aaris III expedition, when his theory on the nature of the alien pictographs was proved wrong, he willingly set aside his own work to assist his colleague, Grigor Tansad. Despite this, Tansad felt that Jelok was jealous that his theory had been proven right. Dedicated to his work, Jelok was angry when Doctor Brunou ordered him to abandon his studies when the expedition came under attack.<ref name="AOA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jelok was introduced in the [[Roleplaying game|RPG adventure]] ''[[Artifact of Aaris]]'', published in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]].<ref name="AOA" /> The character was later referenced in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE" /> The adventure implies that, under the influence of the Plaque of Victory, the members of MS-133 turned upon each other, resulting in multiple deaths and the destruction of the team's shuttle.<ref name="AOA" /> However, ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'', published in [[2008]], confirmed that the team came under attack from the remnants of the Aaris civilization.<ref name="CSWE" />

Regardless, the adventure implies that Jelok was responsible for the murder of Grigor Tansad. Tansad's own notes state that he felt that Jelok was jealous of his work, and Tansad's body was found under the rubble in the chamber in which he and Jelok were working. Tansad had been killed by a blow to the head, and the murder weapon was nearby, still covered in [[Blood/Legends|blood]]. However, since neither source directly confirms these implications, this article only presents the information without assumptions.<ref name="AOA" />
